name: MCP server answers tools/list without authentication
description: POSTs a JSON-RPC tools/list to an MCP server's Streamable-HTTP endpoint with no credentials. When the server returns its tool catalogue to an unauthenticated caller, it discloses the entire tool surface — and, far worse, strongly implies those tools (SQL execution, file access, config mutation) are invocable pre-auth, a confused-deputy condition where the server proxies actions with its own privileges on behalf of any network-adjacent caller. This is the unauthenticated-MCP-server class seen in real advisories (e.g. MCP servers defaulting to 0.0.0.0 with no auth). The predicate looks for a JSON-RPC result carrying a tools array so a 401/empty response doesn't trip it.

vulnerability.remediation: Require authorization on the MCP HTTP transport before answering any method, including tools/list. Implement the MCP authorization spec (OAuth 2.1 bearer tokens on the Streamable-HTTP endpoint) and reject unauthenticated JSON-RPC requests with 401. Bind the server to loopback / a private interface rather than 0.0.0.0 when it is not meant to be network-reachable, and run tool calls with least-privilege credentials — never the server's own broad credentials on behalf of an unauthenticated caller. Verify with `curl -k -X POST https://host/mcp -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/list"}'` — a gated server returns 401, not a tools array.
